name: Salesforce
description: Enterprise CRM for sales, service, and customer data
blocker: Production access still follows org edition, scopes, and admin policy

name: HubSpot
description: CRM and marketing automation platform
blocker: Public distribution requires OAuth and app review

name: Pipedrive
description: Pipeline-focused sales CRM
blocker: Marketplace apps must use OAuth

name: Attio
description: Flexible, data-model-driven CRM
blocker: Multi-workspace apps need OAuth

name: Twenty
description: Open-source CRM with customizable objects
blocker: Self-hosted deployments add version variance

name: Podio
description: Configurable work and relationship management
blocker: Workspace permissions bound each token

name: Zoho CRM
description: Full-suite CRM for sales operations
blocker: Multi-region domains and granular scopes add setup cost

name: Close
description: Sales CRM with calling, email, and automation
blocker: API access requires a Close account; OAuth is for partner apps

name: Copper
description: Google Workspace-centric CRM
blocker: Workspace user email and account permissions are required

name: DealCloud
description: Deal and relationship management for capital markets
blocker: An admin must enable API access for the user group

name: Zendesk
description: Ticketing and customer-service platform
blocker: API-token auth is scheduled for retirement in 2027

name: Intercom
description: Customer messaging, support, and engagement
blocker: Public apps require review and approved scopes

name: Freshdesk
description: Cloud helpdesk and ticketing software
blocker: Official MCP is Enterprise EAP; API limits vary by plan

name: Front
description: Collaborative inbox and customer-operations platform
blocker: Public apps require OAuth and marketplace review

name: Pylon
description: B2B support platform spanning chat and tickets
blocker: Only admins can create scoped API tokens

name: LiveAgent
description: Helpdesk, chat, and call-center software
blocker: API v1 and v3 use separate keys and plan limits

name: Plain
description: API-first B2B customer support platform
blocker: Workspace-scoped keys need careful tenant isolation

name: Help Scout
description: Shared inbox, docs, and customer support
blocker: Docs and Inbox APIs use different auth models

name: Gorgias
description: Ecommerce-focused customer support helpdesk
blocker: Public apps require OAuth and review

name: Gladly
description: Enterprise customer-service conversation platform
blocker: OAuth registration and API roles require Gladly coordination

name: Slack
description: Team messaging and collaboration workspace
blocker: Workspace admin approval can block installation

name: Twilio
description: Programmable messaging, voice, and communications
blocker: Capabilities and regulatory setup vary by country

name: Zoho Cliq
description: Business team chat in the Zoho suite
blocker: Zoho data-center domains must be handled correctly

name: Lark
description: Messaging, documents, calendar, and workplace suite
blocker: Tenant admins approve app permissions

name: Pumble
description: Team messaging and workspace collaboration
blocker: API add-on availability depends on workspace plan

name: Discord
description: Community chat, bots, and real-time messaging
blocker: Privileged gateway intents need approval at scale

name: Telegram
description: Consumer messaging with bot and client APIs
blocker: Bot API and user API are separate trust models

name: WhatsApp Business
description: Business messaging over Meta's WhatsApp network
blocker: Business verification, templates, and app review gate production

name: Aircall
description: Cloud phone system and call-center platform
blocker: Multi-customer OAuth credentials require technology partnership

name: Vonage
description: Programmable voice, video, SMS, and verification
blocker: Each API family has a different signing scheme

name: Google Ads
description: Programmatic campaign management and reporting
blocker: Production developer-token access is reviewed

name: Meta Ads
description: Advertising management across Meta properties
blocker: App Review and business permissions gate real customers

name: LinkedIn Ads
description: Campaign creation and analytics on LinkedIn
blocker: Advertising API products require explicit approval

name: GoHighLevel
description: Agency CRM, funnels, and marketing automation
blocker: Marketplace access and OAuth depend on agency plan

name: Mailchimp
description: Email marketing and audience automation
blocker: Partner listing requires review, but private use is immediate

name: Klaviyo
description: Ecommerce marketing automation and customer data
blocker: Marketplace distribution requires OAuth and review

name: systeme.io
description: Funnels, email, courses, and online-business automation
blocker: Public API availability follows account access

name: Pinterest
description: Visual discovery and advertising platform
blocker: Trial access itself requires application approval

name: Threads
description: Meta's text-based social network
blocker: Non-tester access requires Meta App Review

name: SendGrid

blocker: Sender verification is required before meaningful sends

name: Shopify
description: Hosted commerce platform for online stores
blocker: Public apps need review and version upgrades every quarter

name: WooCommerce

name: BigCommerce

blocker: Marketplace publication requires partner review

name: Salesforce Commerce Cloud

name: Adobe Commerce
description: Composable and self-hosted enterprise ecommerce
blocker: Deployment versions and admin integration setup vary

name: Squarespace
description: Website builder with commerce APIs
blocker: OAuth extensions require registration and review

name: Ecwid
description: Embeddable online-store and commerce platform
blocker: API access needs a paid store or manual dev-store upgrade

name: Gumroad
description: Digital-product storefront and creator payments
blocker: API documentation is sparse and marketplace semantics are limited

name: Amazon Selling Partner
description: Amazon seller operations, catalog, and orders
blocker: Public apps undergo Amazon registration and data-access review

name: Fanbasis
description: Creator storefronts, payments, and digital experiences
blocker: Official API docs are login-gated; no public OAuth path

name: DataForSEO
description: SEO, SERP, and marketing-data APIs
blocker: Usage is credit-based and asynchronous endpoints need polling

name: SE Ranking
description: SEO rank tracking, audit, and competitive research
blocker: API access and quotas depend on subscription

name: Ahrefs
description: SEO backlink, keyword, and site data
blocker: Production integrations and hosted MCP require paid access

name: MrScraper
description: Hosted AI-assisted web scraping
blocker: Targets can block or change independently of the API

name: Apify
description: Cloud platform for scraping and automation actors
blocker: Actor inputs and outputs are not uniform

name: Firecrawl
description: Web crawling and extraction for LLM-ready content
blocker: Credits and target-site defenses constrain reliability

name: Bright Data
description: Proxy, scraping, browser, and web-data platform
blocker: Products expose different endpoints and compliance controls

name: Sherlock

blocker: No hosted API: toolkit must wrap a local process and normalize sites

name: Waterfall.io
description: Contact and company enrichment waterfall
blocker: Master keys and broader access are enterprise-managed

name: Clay
description: Data enrichment and outbound workflow platform
blocker: Broader people/company API access is plan-dependent

name: GitHub
description: Code hosting, collaboration, and software delivery
blocker: Fine-grained permissions and org approval need careful UX

name: Vercel
description: Frontend cloud deployment and hosting platform
blocker: Marketplace provider status is reviewed; private API use is immediate

name: Netlify
description: Web hosting, deployment, forms, and edge services
blocker: Public integrations must implement OAuth

name: Cloudflare
description: Edge network, DNS, security, and developer platform
blocker: The API surface is huge and token permissions are product-specific

name: Supabase
description: Open-source Postgres backend platform
blocker: Service-role credentials must never reach user-facing agents

name: Neo4j
description: Graph database and managed graph platform
blocker: Write queries need strong guardrails

name: Snowflake
description: Enterprise cloud data warehouse and platform
blocker: Admins must configure integrations, roles, and network policy

name: MongoDB Atlas
description: Managed document database and cloud operations
blocker: Legacy keys and service accounts coexist

name: Datadog
description: Observability, monitoring, logs, and incident data
blocker: Multi-customer OAuth is limited to approved partners

name: Sentry
description: Application error monitoring and performance tracing
blocker: Public integrations still require partner review

name: Notion
description: Collaborative docs, wikis, and structured databases
blocker: Pages must be explicitly shared with each integration

name: Airtable
description: Spreadsheet-database hybrid for team workflows
blocker: Base-level permissions and rate limits shape tool design

name: Linear
description: Issue tracking and product development workflow
blocker: GraphQL complexity needs a curated action layer

name: Jira
description: Issue tracking and agile project management
blocker: Cloud and Data Center APIs differ materially

name: Asana
description: Work, task, and project management
blocker: Enterprise service accounts require super-admin setup

name: Monday.com
description: Visual work management and automation platform
blocker: Complexity accounting and board schemas vary

name: ClickUp
description: Tasks, docs, chat, and project management
blocker: Workspace admins can restrict MCP and app installs

name: Coda
description: Collaborative documents with programmable tables
blocker: Pack OAuth and public API tokens are separate paths

name: Smartsheet
description: Enterprise work management in grid form
blocker: API access requires Business or Enterprise plan

name: Harvest
description: Time tracking, expenses, and invoicing
blocker: Only admins can register OAuth applications

name: Stripe
description: Payments, billing, and financial infrastructure
blocker: Use restricted keys and confirmation steps for money movement

name: Plaid
description: Bank-account connectivity and financial data
blocker: Production access and institution OAuth require review

name: Binance
description: Crypto exchange, trading, and market data
blocker: Trading actions need strict scope and confirmation controls

name: Paygent Connect
description: Marketplace payments, onboarding, and payouts

name: iPayX
description: Institutional foreign-exchange audit and verification
blocker: No public developer documentation or credential path was located

name: QuickBooks
description: Small-business accounting, invoices, and payments
blocker: Production apps must pass Intuit review

name: Xero
description: Cloud accounting and bookkeeping platform
blocker: Connection-based pricing and app review affect distribution

name: Brex
description: Corporate cards, spend, travel, and banking
blocker: Customer admin agreement or partner approval is required

name: Ramp
description: Corporate cards, expenses, and accounts payable

name: PitchBook

name: NotebookLM

name: Otter AI

name: Fathom
description: AI meeting recorder, summaries, and call insights

name: Consensus

name: Reducto

name: Devin

name: Higgsfield
description: AI image and video generation suite
blocker: Generation is asynchronous and credit-intensive

name: YouTube Transcript

name: Grain
